title: On Triple Product L-Functions
speaker: Miao Gu
abstract: The Poisson summation conjecture of Braverman-Kazhdan, L. Lafforgue, Ngo, and Sakellaridis is an ambitious proposal to prove analytic properties of quite general Langlands L-functions using vast generalizations of the Poisson summation formula. In this talk, we introduce multivariable zeta integrals which unfold to Euler products representing the triple product L-functions times a product of L-functions with known analytic properties. We then formulate a generalization of the Poisson summation conjecture and show it implies the expected analytic properties of triple product L-functions. Finally, we propose a strategy, the fiber bundle method, to reduce this generalized conjecture to a case of the Poisson summation conjecture in which spectral methods can be employed together with certain local compatibility statements. This talk is based on joint work with Jayce Getz, Chun-Hsien Hsu, and Spencer Leslie.

proof_stages.label: Poisson-summation template for automorphic L-functions
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from classical Poisson summation and the Godement-Jacquet zeta integral to establish the guiding paradigm that a Fourier transform plus a Poisson formula can produce analytic continuation and functional equations of L-functions.

proof_stages.label: Braverman-Kazhdan and spherical-variety Poisson program
proof_stages.summary: The speaker places the work in the conjectural Braverman-Kazhdan framework, where a spherical variety should have a Schwartz space, a Fourier transform, a basic function, and a Poisson summation formula, while emphasizing that global cases remain scarce.

proof_stages.label: Triple-product L-function target
proof_stages.summary: The automorphic data are introduced as three cuspidal representations of general linear groups, with the desired L-function being the tensor-product/triple-product L-function whose higher-rank analytic properties are not covered by known functorial transfers.

proof_stages.label: Geometric spaces and group actions for the new integral
proof_stages.summary: The construction defines open schemes X and Y, together with the relevant GL_r, GL_{r-2}, and product-of-GL_2 actions, in a form engineered to support a nonzero triple-product zeta integral.

proof_stages.label: Theta kernel, global zeta integral, and unfolding
proof_stages.summary: Using Schwartz functions on the constructed space and additive characters/pairings, the speaker defines theta-type sums and a global integral against cusp forms, then unfolds it to an Eulerian local integral.

proof_stages.label: Unramified local calculation and L-factor identification
proof_stages.summary: At unramified places the local zeta integral with spherical Whittaker data and a naive basic function is computed to be the desired triple-product L-factor divided by known Rankin-Selberg and standard factors, up to a controlled error expected to disappear for the true basic function.

proof_stages.label: Conditional analytic consequences for triple products
proof_stages.summary: Assuming the required Poisson summation conjecture, the correct basic functions, and local compatibility statements, the functional equation of the zeta integrals is intended to yield meromorphic continuation and functional equations for the triple-product L-functions.

proof_stages.label: Fiber-bundle method for the needed Poisson formula
proof_stages.summary: To attack the conjectural Poisson formula for the constructed space, the speaker describes a fiber-bundle reduction: prove Poisson summation on fibers, handle vector-space fibers by known Fourier analysis and singular fibers by a spectral/local Fourier-transform method, then assemble the global statement from local data.

examples.name: Classical Poisson summation on a vector space
examples.note: The speaker reviews the Fourier transform on a real vector space with lattice and the identity summing a Schwartz function over rational/integral points, as the elementary model for the later automorphic Poisson formulas.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker sets up the vector-space case with lattice, Schwartz functions, Fourier transform integral, and the explicit Poisson summation formula

examples.note: The zeta integral on the matrix space for GL_n is used as a standard illustration of how a Poisson summation formula gives meromorphic continuation and a functional equation for an automorphic L-function.

examples.name: Riemann zeta boundary terms in Poisson summation
examples.note: An audience question invokes the familiar need to remove or handle the zero locus in Riemann's proof, illustrating that boundary conditions are a real issue in spherical-variety Poisson formulas.
